page_content:
ANTH647 Advanced Material Culture Studies in Archaeology (3 Credits) An in-depth introduction to the world of material culture studies with a focus on the methods and theories in historical archaeology. Students will look at archaeological data as historical documents, commodities and as symbols expressing ideas. Credit Only Granted for: ANTH447, ANTH448C, ANTH647, or ANTH689C. Formerly: ANTH689C.

ANTH649 Advanced Developments in Archaeology (3 Credits) Graduate Archaeology courses on varying topics derived from new interests of the faculty or the specialties of visiting scholars. Restriction: Permission of BSOS-Anthropology department. Repeatable to: 6 credits if content differs.

ANTH651 Environmental Archaeology (3 Credits) An overview of modern environmental archaeology as a tool for the interdisciplinary investigation of past and present global change and to engage the long term past with current issues of sustainability and rapid environmental change. Credit Only Granted for: ANTH448F, ANTH451, ANTH688F, or ANTH651. Formerly: ANTH688F.
